content: The relative performance gaps between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and standalone curious agents can be explained by how each method leverages unlabeled environmental memories differently, aligning with 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory design principles. We can hypothesize that curious agents that explicitly integrate SOTA differentiable episodic memory compression from active learning will outperform both ICM and RND on hard-exploration sparse-reward tasks by reducing redundant memory of predictable states.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
